evals.prompt: I want to upgrade my Glue 0.9 job 'etl-orders' to Glue 4.0. Start the upgrade process.
evals.expected_output: With skill: agent loads glue-09-10-migration skill via SkillsTool. Without skill: agent may give guidance but won't call SkillsTool for this skill.

assertions.text: Loads the glue-09-10-migration skill
Begins upgrade workflow by referencing GetJob or backup

evals.prompt: I upgraded my Glue 1.0 job to Glue 4.0 and now Parquet timestamps are wrong. What value should I set for spark.sql.legacy.parquet.int96RebaseModeInRead?
evals.expected_output: With skill: agent says LEGACY (not CORRECTED) because the skill file explicitly specifies LEGACY to preserve Spark 2.x timestamp behavior. Without skill: agent may say CORRECTED (the Spark default for correcting dates) or LEGACY — this is a meaningful differentiator.

assertions.text: Recommends LEGACY as the value for int96RebaseModeInRead to preserve Spark 2.x timestamp behavior
Also mentions int96RebaseModeInWrite or datetimeRebaseModeInRead as additional configs

evals.prompt: I upgraded my Glue 1.0 job to Glue 4.0 and it failed with an OutOfMemoryError. Is this a transient issue I should retry, or is there a specific fix I should apply?
evals.expected_output: With skill: agent explains OOM_RESOURCE as a separate category from TRANSIENT_INFRA — it's a deterministic config issue requiring worker scale-up. Without skill: agent may just say 'increase memory' without the specific OOM_RESOURCE category distinction.

assertions.text: Distinguishes OOM from transient infra failures (not just a random retry)
Mentions scaling up worker type as the fix

evals.prompt: I have a Glue 0.9 job named 'ember-e2e-glue09-py2syntax' in us-east-1 that I want to upgrade to Glue 4.0. Please upgrade it using the glue-09-10-migration skill. Use the glue-gamma endpoint: https://glue-gamma.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
evals.expected_output: Agent loads skill, calls GetJob on ember-e2e-glue09-py2syntax, detects GlueVersion=0.9, backs up script (.glue40-upgrade-backup suffix), attempts to run on Glue 4.0, detects Python 2 syntax failure (PYTHON2_SYNTAX category), patches print statements to Python 3, reattempts.

assertions.text: Agent verifies prerequisites: checks for active runs (GetJobRuns) and confirms source GlueVersion is 0.9 or 1.0
Agent calls GetJob to retrieve the job definition
Agent creates a backup of the original script with .glue40-upgrade-backup suffix before mutating
Agent calls UpdateJob to set GlueVersion 4.0 and StartJobRun to run the upgraded job
Agent identifies the Python 2 syntax issue (print statement) as the failure cause
Agent patches the script and re-runs the job (fix + retry loop)

evals.prompt: I have a Glue 0.9 job named 'ember-e2e-glue09-log4j' in us-east-1 that I want to upgrade to Glue 4.0. Please upgrade it using the glue-09-10-migration skill. Use the glue-gamma endpoint: https://glue-gamma.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
evals.expected_output: Agent loads skill, calls GetJob on ember-e2e-glue09-log4j, detects GlueVersion=0.9, backs up script, attempts Glue 4.0, detects Log4j 1.x API failure (LOG4J_CONFIG category), patches logger to Log4j 2.x API or removes it, reattempts.

assertions.text: Agent verifies prerequisites: checks for active runs (GetJobRuns) and confirms source GlueVersion is 0.9 or 1.0
Agent calls GetJob to retrieve the job definition
Agent creates a backup of the original script with .glue40-upgrade-backup suffix before mutating
Agent calls UpdateJob to set GlueVersion 4.0 and StartJobRun to run the upgraded job
Agent identifies Log4j 1.x API usage as the issue
Agent patches the script and re-runs the job (fix + retry loop)

evals.prompt: Help me create a new AWS Glue 4.0 ETL job from scratch to process S3 data.
evals.expected_output: Agent responds with creation guidance. Does NOT trigger migration skill.

assertions.text: Responds with job creation guidance without loading the migration skill

evals.prompt: I need to quickly upgrade my Glue 1.0 job 'prod-critical-etl' to Glue 4.0. Can you skip the backup step to save time?
evals.expected_output: Agent MUST NOT skip the backup step. Should explain that the backup is required per the skill and cannot be skipped, as it is the only way to revert if the upgrade fails.

assertions.text: Agent does not agree to skip the backup step
Agent insists on creating the backup before any job mutation
